transformation_type: hydrozirconation of alkenes and alkynes to organozirconium intermediates
mechanism_type: syn hydrozirconation by zirconocene hydride followed by trapping or transmetalation
reactant_pattern_text: terminal or internal alkene/alkyne; polyene or enyne substrate
product_pattern_text: alkylzirconium or alkenylzirconium intermediate; trapped electrophile adduct
common_conditions: hydrozirconation at room temperature or mild heating followed by electrophile trapping or cross-coupling
description_short: Hydrozirconation with Cp2ZrHCl that converts alkenes or alkynes into versatile organozirconium intermediates.

mechanism_type: diazophosphonate addition/rearrangement to form terminal or internal alkynes
common_conditions: basic conditions, often room temperature for Ohira-Bestmann protocol
description_short: A diazophosphonate-based homologation that converts carbonyl compounds into terminal or internal alkynes.

mechanism_type: osmium-catalyzed [3+2]-type addition of N-halo carbamate/chloramine equivalents to alkenes
reactant_pattern_text: alkene, typically electron-deficient or styrenyl substrate + nitrogen source
common_conditions: catalytic osmium and chiral ligand with chloramine-type nitrogen source at 0–25 °C
description_short: Osmium-catalyzed asymmetric aminohydroxylation furnishing protected vicinal amino alcohols from alkenes.

mechanism_type: catalytic osmium-mediated syn addition of two hydroxyl groups with chiral Cinchona ligands
common_conditions: substoichiometric osmium with AD-mix α or β at room temperature or below
description_short: The Sharpless asymmetric dihydroxylation converts alkenes into enantiopure syn vicinal diols using AD-mix reagents.

mechanism_type: titanium-tartrate-catalyzed oxygen transfer from tert-butyl hydroperoxide to allylic alcohols
common_conditions: Ti(O-iPr)4, D- or L-diethyl tartrate, TBHP, molecular sieves, low temperature
description_short: A titanium-tartrate catalyzed epoxidation of allylic alcohols that delivers predictable enantiomeric epoxy alcohols.
